reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–O bond rearrangement in nitrile oxide generation; loss of H2O from ethyl nitroacetate

reagents: Phenylacetylene (1.0 equiv, 0.5 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Dipolarophile substrate dissolved in DES solvent

reagents: Ethyl nitroacetate (2.0 equiv, 1.0 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Nitrile oxide precursor dissolved in DES; kept separate until mixing point to ensure rapid, uniform mixing and minimize premature nitrile oxide dimerization before encountering dipolarophile

reagents.name: Phenylacetylene
reagents.notes: Terminal alkyne serving as 2π component in [3+2] cycloaddition

reagents.name: Ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive reaction to completion

reagents.name: Tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Forms deep eutectic solvent with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium promoting the cycloaddition

reagents.name: Ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES; may assist in nitrile oxide generation via proton shuttle

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) with loss of water, facilitated by the DES medium
Regioselective [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate

key_intermediate: Ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (in-situ generated nitrile oxide from ethyl nitroacetate)

stream_logic.reasoning: Dipolarophile in DES; kept separate from nitrile oxide precursor to ensure mixing occurs at the heated zone, minimizing premature nitrile oxide generation and dimerization at room temperature
Nitrile oxide precursor in DES; 2.0 equiv relative to phenylacetylene; separate stream allows precise stoichiometric control via flow rate ratio

mixing_order_reasoning: Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate should be mixed at a T-mixer immediately before entering the heated coil reactor. This ensures the nitrile oxide intermediate is generated in the presence of the dipolarophile, minimizing competitive nitrile oxide dimerization to furoxan. The DES is viscous (~50-100 cP at RT) so preheating feed lines to ~60°C may be needed to reduce viscosity and ensure good mixing at the T-junction. At 120°C in the reactor, viscosity drops substantially.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; reaction proceeds under air in batch protocol
quench_reasoning: Reaction self-terminates upon consumption of nitrile oxide; batch workup (water quench, DCM extraction) is for product isolation, not quenching a reactive intermediate
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide [3+2] cycloaddition). The DES viscosity at room temperature is a practical concern for flow — may need preheated feed lines or dilution. The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests incomplete nitrile oxide generation efficiency; flow may improve this. Uncertain whether the DES can be pumped reliably at RT through standard HPLC pumps due to high viscosity (~50-100 cP); syringe pumps or gear pumps may be needed. The exact mechanism of nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate (whether purely thermal elimination of H2O or DES-mediated) is somewhat debated but the thermal pathway is well-supported.

intensification_mandate.flow_justification_basis: Flow provides: (1) Superior heat transfer — rapid heating to 120°C in narrow-bore tubing (1 mm ID, surface-to-volume ratio ~4000 m⁻¹ vs ~100 m⁻¹ in batch vial) enables faster temperature equilibration and potentially shorter residence time (target ~5 min vs 15 min batch). (2) Selectivity improvement — precise mixing at T-junction ensures nitrile oxide is always generated in presence of dipolarophile, suppressing furoxan dimerization side product and potentially improving yield beyond 83%. (3) Continuous processing of viscous DES medium with back-pressure regulation enables operation above solvent boiling points. (4) Safe handling of transient nitrile oxide intermediate in small holdup volumes.
